compiled_state.title: As a project operator, onboarding writes stack-specific defaults for commands.lint, commands.format, and commands.format_check
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update onboarding SKILL.md Phase 3 Step 2b to write commands.lint, commands.format, and commands.format_check per detected stack (Python/Node.js/Ruby). Implement key-presence preservation: if key already set, emit [DSO INFO] commands.<key> already set — skipping and leave value unchanged. Step ordering must place commands.test_runner (Epic A) write before these writes.

**Why**: Without onboarding defaults, operators must manually configure all three keys for non-Python projects, undermining the epic's goal of zero-config stack detection.

**Scope**:
- IN: onboarding SKILL.md Phase 3 Step 2b changes; per-stack defaults for Python/Node.js/Ruby; key-presence preservation semantics; [DSO INFO] signal; step ordering
- OUT: validate.sh/auto-format.sh/gate changes (covered by S1-S3 and S6), multi-stack detection logic (follow Epic A patterns for stack detection order)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, onboarding on a Ruby project sets commands.lint=bundle exec rubocop, commands.format=bundle exec rubocop --autocorrect, commands.format_check=bundle exec rubocop
  ← Satisfies: "Onboarding writes stack-specific defaults for Ruby"
- When this story is complete, onboarding on a Node/JS project sets commands.lint=eslint --no-error-on-unmatched-pattern, commands.format=prettier --write, commands.format_check=prettier --check
  ← Satisfies: "Onboarding writes stack-specific defaults for Node/JS"
- When this story is complete, re-onboarding on a project with existing commands.lint emits [DSO INFO] commands.lint already set — skipping and leaves value unchanged
  ← Satisfies: "Re-onboarding preserves existing values; emits [DSO INFO] per preserved key"
- When this story is complete, onboarding step ordering places commands.test_runner (Epic A) write before commands.lint/format/format_check writes
  ← Satisfies: "Epic A (87e9-0bc8) sequencing dependency"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing covering: Ruby defaults, Node/JS defaults, key-presence skip, empty-string key treated as not-set

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Onboarding step ordering must ensure Epic A (87e9-0bc8) commands.test_runner write precedes commands.lint/format/format_check writes; if Epic A has not landed, this story is blocked
- [Testing] Key-presence check must reject empty-string values as not-set — test this edge case explicitly
- [Conflicting Assumptions] For polyglot repos (e.g., Ruby backend + JS frontend), follow Epic A's multi-stack detection patterns as the source of truth for which stack wins

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
